PART 3 EX
ECUTION
3.01 EXAMINATION / PREPARATION
A. EHS Staff Duties
1. 1. The Contractor’s EHS staff (SP, EP and EHSR) are responsible for
overseeing and managing the Contractor’s safe and environmentally
compliant performance of all Work.
2. EHS Professional(s)shall be required to initiate, review and implement
measures to ensure the health and safety of all Contractor employees, and to
protect property and the environment.  Each EHS Professional is required to
visit the Site and audit the Site conditions, at a minimum monthly, in
accordance with the Contractor’s EHS Plan or as directed by the Engineer.
3. The EHS Professional will be held accountable to adjust his/her workload to
enable proper performance of all of their EHS responsibilities in accordance
with all requirements of this Section and all applicable regulations. DEP may
request that the Contractor remove the EHS Professional for not meeting the
Contract requirements.
4. The SP shall visit the Site prior to developing the Contractor’s EHASP. The
SP will arrange a visit with the Engineer and perform an inspection of the
Site to understand the full scope of Work to be performed under the Contract.
Contract Documents relevant to writing the EHASP can be reviewed and
obtained at this time. Facility/Site specific information must be provided,
reviewed, and documented in accordance with the BEDC EHS Site
Orientation Standard.
5. The EHS Professionals shall visit all Work areas as frequently as necessary,
but no less frequently than monthly, to verify that EHS compliance is being
achieved.  The EHS Professionals shall review hazards, JHAs, and the
foremen’s and superintendent’s preparation and communication of JHAs to
432 of 772
SECTION 01 35 27 – ENVIRONMENTAL, HEALTH AND SAFETY
REQUIREMENTS
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 19 of 36
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
workers.  The EHS Professionals shall also review the Project team’s
compliance with, and adherence to EHS requirements, as well as their
proactive approach and planning for EHS.
6. The EHS Professionals shall be available for consultation whenever
necessary.  Prior to and after each visit, the EHS Professional shall sign the
visitors’ log maintained at the Engineer’s office.
7. The EHS Professionals are expected to perform their inspections in concert
with the EHS Rep, during which time the EHS Professional will not only
inspect the Site, but shall also mentor and direct the EHS Rep. During the
inspection, the EHS Professional will evaluate the Contractor and each
subcontractor working under the Contract and clearly identify findings and
who they are assigned to, using the SP/EP checklist provided in the EHASP.
Any findings that cannot be resolved immediately will be assigned to the
EHS Rep for follow-up. The EHS Professionals are expected to communicate
with the EHS Rep to ensure all identified findings are closed out.  The EHS
Professionals’ inspection shall include programmatic issues such as adhering
to the Contractor’s EHS program, including, but not limited to, preparing and
communicating JHAs and proactively minimizing EHS risks.
8. The EHS Rep will coordinate with the EHS Professionals when questions
arise requiring the EHS Professional’s expertise.  After each visit, the EHS
Professional shall prepare a report, including photographs (where necessary),
acceptable to the Engineer and BEDC EHS, detailing the findings.  The
report shall include those hazards and violations discovered during the site
visit and when and how they were or will be closed out. Any EHS items not
covered or documented by the inspection checklist will be noted in the
comments section of the checklist.  The report shall be submitted to the
Engineer within one business day of the Site visit.
9. The ESP shall be at the Site full time during all shifts whenever electrical
work is in progress.  The ESP is responsible for electrical safety planning and
enforcing Contractor compliance with electrical safe work practices.
10. The EHS Rep shall be at the job Site full time whenever work is in progress
during all shifts.
11. The SP’s  and EHSR’s responsibilities are as follows:
a. The SP is responsible for directing the Contractor’s EHS program,
ensuring implementation by the Contractor and all Subcontractors, and
for directing and monitoring all activities of the EHS Rep. If the EHS
Rep is not enforcing the EHASP, JHAs, or other elements of the EHS
program, the SP shall either recommend retraining or removal of the
EHS Rep from the Project by submitting a letter to the Engineer and the
Contractor.
433 of 772
SECTION 01 35 27 – ENVIRONMENTAL, HEALTH AND SAFETY
REQUIREMENTS
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 20 of 36
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
b. The EHS Rep must review JHAs to verify that the Work activity’s EHS
issues and hazards are accurately identified, addressed, and
communicated. JHAs shall be regularly communicated to affected
employees and must be made available in the areas where the affected
employees are working.  The EHS Professionals are responsible for
reviewing JHAs at least monthly to verify that they adequately reflect
the recognized hazards and controls of the tasks being performed.
JHAs shall be provided to the Engineer or BEDC EHS when requested.
c. Both the SP amd EHSR shall schedule and conduct EHS meetings and
training programs as required. A specific schedule of these meetings
and an outline of topics to be covered shall be provided with the
EHASP. The Engineer shall be advised in advance of the time and
place of such meetings, and DEP personnel shall be invited to attend
the meetings.
d. All Contractor employees shall be instructed by the SP and the EHS
Rep on the recognition of hazards, safe Work practices and
environmental precautions, the contents of the EHASP, and the use of
environmental, personal protective and emergency equipment.  Such
training shall be documented, recorded, and provided as part of the
EHS monthly report(s).  EHSRs and EHS Professionals will attend
regularly scheduled meetings held by the Engineer and BEDC EHS
including, but not limited to, the EHS Pre-Construction Meeting.
e. Determine that operators of specific equipment are qualified by
training, certification and/or experience before they are allowed to
operate such equipment.  Ensure documentation of licenses,
certifications and training by the appropriate agencies (for example
OSHA, NYC DOB, NYC Fire Department, etc.) are on site and current,
prior to start of Work.
f. Verify implementation of the BEDC Emergency Action Plan (EAP)
Standard.
g. Develop an effective Site communication plan that includes, signage,
and verbal and written communication of EHS issues and notices.
h. Post all appropriate notices regarding EHS regulations at Site
location(s) which afford maximum exposure to all personnel at the job
Site.
i. Post appropriate instructions and warning signs regarding all hazardous
areas or conditions which cannot be eliminated. Identification of these
areas shall be based on experience, site surveillance, and severity of
hazard.  Such signs shall not be used in place of appropriate workplace
controls.
434 of 772
SECTION 01 35 27 – ENVIRONMENTAL, HEALTH AND SAFETY
REQUIREMENTS
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 21 of 36
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
j. The EHS Rep shall conduct EHS inspections a minimum of twice per
shift to ensure that all machines, tools and equipment are in a safe
operating condition, and that all Work areas are free of safety and
environmental hazards. The EHS Rep shall take necessary and
immediate corrective actions, where feasible, to eliminate all unsafe
acts and/or conditions, and submit to the Engineer each day a copy of
the findings on the inspection check list report forms established in the
EHASP.  Detailed checklists shall be tailored to the EHS hazards and
conditions on the Site, and shall include a comments section to include
findings not specifically listed on the checklist.
k. Whenever DEP and its agents perform both announced and
unannounced inspections of the Contractor’s EHS performance, a
member of the Contractor’s on-site EHS team shall be available during
the inspections.  The SP will coordinate inspections with the Engineer
and DEP inspectors upon request. The Contractor shall take immediate
corrective action, where feasible, to eliminate hazards identified by the
Engineer, DEP inspectors, or any other entity.  The Contractor, if
requested, shall develop and implement a plan detailing corrective
actions necessary to mitigate the presence of noncompliant conditions
and actions following Incidents, citations, NOVs, or identification of
patterns of noncompliant conditions and acts.
l. Notify the Engineer and BEDC EHS immediately of all inspections by
regulatory agencies, and submit to the Engineer and BEDC EHS copies
of all EHS reports, citations, and NOVs from regulatory agencies and
insurance companies within one workday of receipt.
m. Implement an effective fire protection and prevention program at the
Site throughout all phases of the construction Work in accordance with
BEDC’s Fire Prevention Standard.  The Contractor will ensure the
availability of fire protection and suppression equipment adequate to
control the degree of fire hazard encountered during construction.
n. Provide and document appropriate Site-specific orientation to
Contractor employees, visitors, and Subcontractors communicating
recognized hazards present at and surrounding the Site(s) and facility in
accordance with BEDC’s Site Orientation Standard and DEP’s
Contractor Selection and Management Policy.
o. Perform all tasks and responsibilities as identified in the EHASP.

A. Not Used

3.03 FIELD TESTING / QUALITY CONTROL
A. EPCRA and Related Hazardous Material Regulations
1. The Contractor shall maintain a monthly inventory of hazardous substances
or extremely hazardous substances used or stored on Site in accordance with
the BEDC-provided chemical inventory form.  Documentation shall be
maintained on Site and available for review.
2. The Contractor shall prepare and provide to the Engineer a Right to Know
(RTK)/Emergency Planning and Community Right to Know Act (EPCRA)
Annual Chemical Inventory Form for all hazardous and extremely hazardous
substances that the Contractor used or stored during the previous calendar
year.  This shall be provided by January 15th of each year.
3. In addition to any requirements of the New York City Standard Construction
Contract, in order to obtain Final Acceptance, the Contractor must satisfy all
EPCRA reporting requirements for the final year of the Contract.
B. Temporary Protective Grounding Equipment
1. Temporary Protective Grounding Equipment (TPGE) shall be used at
locations required by 29 CFR 1910.269.  TPGE may be used at other
locations, when specified in the EHASP.
2. TPGE shall not be used as a substitute for following lockout procedures.
C. Hot Work
1. The performance of Hot Work is prohibited unless performed under the
issuance of a Hot Work permit from the Permit Authorizing Individual (PAI).
The PAI shall be provided by the DEP.
a. Where the DEP and Engineer are not routinely present, the Contractor
may be allowed to obtain PAI certification for Contract employees.
2. The Contractor shall provide the Engineer with a JHA at least 48 hours prior
to the performance of Hot Work. The JHA shall identify the following:
a. The locations where the Work is to performed;
b. Equipment to be used;
c. Gases or vapors within or adjacent to the location that have the
potential to create a combustible atmosphere;
d. Controls required to mitigate or prevent the accumulation of gases or
vapors in quantities that have the potential to create a combustible
atmosphere; and
e. Controls or methods to promptly discontinue the use of and de-energize
equipment when a combustible atmosphere is detected.
436 of 772
SECTION 01 35 27 – ENVIRONMENTAL, HEALTH AND SAFETY
REQUIREMENTS
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 23 of 36
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
3. For Hot Work in hazardous (classified) locations, refer to the requirements of

### Section 01 35 44 – Working in Hazardous (Classified) Locations.

D. Electrical Equipment Lockout
1. Contractor is required to lock out all electrical equipment utilizing lockable
disconnecting means that provide mechanical isolation followed by
verification of no voltage, as according to OHSA 1910/1926 and NFPA 70E;
equipment tag out is not sufficient.  If a lockable disconnecting means is not
available at the identified equipment due to equipment design or operational
condition, the Lockout process will continue elevating to the next level of the
system, electrical or otherwise, until a lockable disconnecting means is
available providing mechanical isolation. Complex electrical systems and
network power distribution systems require lockable disconnecting means at
all isolation points from the main power source through the downstream
equipment loads (e.g. systems with A and B bus sources or other multiple
power sources).  The lock out requirement will be performed to the greatest
extent possible in consideration of plant operational impacts. When the plant
operational impacts are too significant to environmental compliance, an
alternative method may be utilized know as an Energized Electrical Work
Permit (EEWP).  An EEWP is a document, defined by NFPA 70E, that
clearly describes the following: the circuit, equipment, and location of the
job/task at hand; the work that is to be done; justification of why the circuit
or equipment cannot be de-energized or the work deferred until the next
scheduled outage; performed by an Electrical Safety Worker (ESW);
signatory authorization by the Plant Chief or Facility Manager.
E. Operability Assessment Program
1. An Operability Assessment Program as defined in Section XX XX XX will
be performed to verify the proper mechanical operation and lockout
disconnecting means of the power distribution system equipment within the
contract work scope.  This program would start at the construction NTP and
will be completed as the first contractor work activity.  This program would
need to be completed for each work area prior to any electrical work being
performed.  The program checklist for each unit of equipment will require
sign-off by the Contractor ESP, CM, and Plant Chief.
3.04 STARTUP / DEMONSTRATION
A. Visitors
1. Allowing visitors and members of the public to tour an active construction
site is discouraged due to the potential exposures to hazardous conditions and
materials associated with construction Work.  However, where necessary,
approved and authorized visitors of the Contractor, Subcontractors, or any
other authorized agency, department, or other entity associated with the

evacuation drills.  Visitors must not be exposed to construction hazards

costs for Work required by this Section shall be included in the applicable lump sum, unit price(s)
or allowance(s) as set forth in Section 01 27 00 – Measurement and Payment.

SECTION 01 35 45 – HAZARDOUS MATERIALS CONTROL
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452 E

Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
1.03 RELATED SECTIONS
A. Section 01 27 00 – Measurement and Payment

1.04 REFERENCES
A. Definitions
1. Competent Person: One who is capable of identifying existing and predictable hazards in
the work area or unsanitary, hazardous, or dangerous working conditions, and who has
authority to take prompt corrective measures (29 CFR 1926.32(f)).
2. Large Quantity Generator (LQG): A facility that generates (in a calendar month) ≥1,000
kg of total hazardous waste or >1 kg of acute hazardous waste or >100 kg of acute
hazardous waste spill residue or soil, or at any time stores hazardous and acute hazard ous
wastes in greater quantities (at any time) than 6,000 kg of hazardous waste, 1 kg acute
hazardous waste, or 100 kg of acute hazardous waste spill residue or soil.
1.05 DESCRIPTION
A. Known Hazardous Materials
1. There are materials present within the designated Work areas that will require special
handling and other safeguard measures in order to minimize chemical exposure hazards to
site workers and to prevent environmental impacts to offsite areas. These materials are
primarily lead and mercury containing paints/coatings on various components and
substrates. Summary tables of materials tested are included at the end of this Section. A
Hazardous Materials Mitigation report was prepared for the project and is included as part
of the project documents.  As applicable to its Work, the Contractor shall incorporate these
minimum requirements into its Environmental, Health and Safety Plan (EHASP) or other
applicable submittal to ensure a safe and healthful working environment. The EHASP shall
be designed in accordance with Section 01 35 27 – Environmental, Health and Safety
Requirements.
a. Upon completion of any additional material sampling at the W ork Site(s), the
Contractor’s EHASP shall be updated as needed to incorporate new data
generated by analysis of the samples.
2. The Contractor shall not initiate or proceed with any Work in areas associated with the
contaminated, potentially hazardous, or hazardous materials until these materials have been
removed from these areas or managed in accordance with the DEP Hazardous Was te
Management Policy and the following Specifications:
a. Asbestos-containing material shall be handled in accordance with Section 02 82
05 – Asbestos Management. No samples collected and analyzed as part of the
design investigations were identified as asbestos containing materials; Note the
roof system at the Harlem Railroad Siphon Chamber is presumed asbestos
452 of 772
SECTION 01 35 45 – HAZARDOUS MATERIALS CONTROL
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452 E
CAT-452  Section Page 3 of 22
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
containing until proven otherwise by NYSDOL certified inspectors and analyzed
by a NYSDOH ELAP approved laboratory
b. No mercury containing equipment was identified during the design investigations.
Note, select paints are identified as mercury containing (refer to Table 3) .
Mercury-containing paint shall be handled in accordance with Section 02 83 05 –
Lead Management as paint containing other heavy metals.
c. Lead -containing paint shall be handled in accordance with Section 02 83 05 –
Lead Management . Lead containing paint and materials are identified on the
majority of components and substrates of the Chambers (refer to Table 2).
d. No PCB containing equipment or paints were identified during the design
investigations.
e. If not handled under the above Sections, universal waste and other regulated
wastes shall be handled in accordance with Section 01 74 20 – Construction Waste
Management. Bat guano shall be handled in accordance with Section 01 57 16
Temporary Pest Control . Universal wastes, electronic waste, and miscellaneous
hazardous materials are identified or presumed present at the project Chambers
(refer to Table 4).
3. The Contractor shall attend an initial site inspection, and any other meetings to review
hazardous materials control issues in connection with the progress of the Work. The initial
site inspection shall be as described below. Other meetings to monitor hazardous materials
control issues associated with the Work, including any briefing of DEP personnel,
including, but not limited to, facility managers and supervisors, shall be scheduled as
necessary.
a. Initial Site Inspection:  Within fi ve (5) business days after approval of the
environmental health and safety (EHS) Resources, or an agreed upon timeframe,
the Contractor, the Engineer, and applicable DEP personnel shall perform an
initial site inspection to review all the Work areas that will be affected by
contaminated, potentially hazardous and hazardous materials.  EHS Resources are
defined in Section 01 35 27 – Environmental Health and Safety Requirements.
1) Initial site inspection shall be organized by the Contractor who shall
contact DEP, and the Engineer to determine the representatives that
should attend.
2) A minimum of one representative from each party shall be present for
the inspection.
3) Competent Persons shall lead the site inspection.
4) Sampling outside of the Work to be performed may not be performed
without DEP approval.
5) A site inspection report shall be distributed by the Contractor at the
beginning of the site inspection. The site inspection report shall include
drawing(s) and associated text that describes the work in sufficient detail
to aid in the site inspection.
6) The Contractor shall note in the site inspection report any special
requirements that they have to perform their Work during the inspection.
453 of 772
SECTION 01 35 45 – HAZARDOUS MATERIALS CONTROL
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452 E
CAT-452  Section Page 4 of 22
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
7) The Contractor shall prepare and distribute a summary of the site
inspection and any comments noted during the inspection to all parties
present at the site inspection.
8) In the event that the initial site inspection does not satisfactorily identify
the contaminated, potentially hazardous, and hazardous materials
potentially affecting the Work, follow -up inspection(s) shall be
organized and held as required.
B. Unforeseen Hazardous Material
1. The Work Sites may contain unforeseen hazardous materials. When a potentially
hazardous material that was previously unforeseen is discovered or an upgrade of its
EHASP is necessary for managing unforeseen hazardous material, the Engineer will direct
the Contractor to engage the services of a hazardous materials specialist to perform the
necessary investigation, develop a remediation plan, and perform the remediation wor k.
Additionally, the Engineer will direct the Contractor to update its EHASP as necessary.
2. The Contractor shall be responsible for identifying previously unknown and suspect
hazardous materials as they are encountered via implementation of the approved sampling
plan. Indication of the presence of hazardous materials, including but not limited to odorous
or stained soils, sediment or liquids, mercury sources and suspect asbestos -containing
materials must be immediately reported to the Engineer. All W ork in the area shall stop
until otherwise directed by the Engineer.
3. In the event that hazardous material is detected, the Engineer will provide the Contractor
with a scope of work for the remediation services and direct the Contractor to obtain cost
proposals for such work from at least three (3) hazardous material special ists unless
otherwise required by the DEP depending upon the magnitude and timing of the work. The
Contractor shall submit the proposals, indicating which hazardous material specialist the
Contractor proposes to engage, to the Engineer within ten (10) business days of receiving
the scope of remediation work. The Engineer shall review the proposals and approve such
selection or direct the Contractor to submit an alternative selection or obtain additional
proposals. Remediation work shall not commence until the Contractor receives written
notice from the Engineer to proceed with the work. As directed by the Engineer, pre -
remediation inspections and coordination may also be required, in a manner similar to the
procedures for known hazardous materials.
4. Soils testing and analysis shall be performed in accordance with Section 02 24 20 - Soil
Sampling and Analysis. In the event that hazardous levels of lead are detected in soil, the
DEP will provide the Contractor with a Community Air Monitoring Program (CAMP) to
include particulate air monitoring.  The Engineer will direct the Contractor to obtain cost
proposals for work of the CAMP from at least three (3) experienced firms unless otherwise
required by the DEP depending upon the magnitude and timing of the work.  Within five
(5) business days of receiving the CAMP, the Contractor shall submit the cost proposals to
the Engineer, indicating which firm the Contractor proposes to engage.  The Engineer shall
review the cost proposals and approve such selection or direct the Contractor to submit an
alternative selection or obtain additional proposals.  CAMP work shall not commence until
the Contractor receives written notice from the Engineer to proceed with the work.
5. The Contractor shall not initiate or proceed with any other Work in areas associated with
contaminated, potentially hazardous, or hazardous materials until these materials have been
removed from these areas or managed, in accordance with the following Specifications:
a. Asbestos-containing material shall be handled in accordance with Section 02 82
05 – Asbestos Management.
454 of 772
SECTION 01 35 45 – HAZARDOUS MATERIALS CONTROL
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452 E
CAT-452  Section Page 5 of 22
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
b. Mercury-containing paint shall be handled in accordance with Section 02 83 05 –
Lead Management as paint containing other heavy metals.
c. Lead -containing paint shall be handled in accordance with Section 02 83 05 –
Lead Management.
d. If not handled under the above Sections, universal waste and other regulated
wastes shall be handled in accordance with Section 01 74 20 – Construction Waste
Management.
6. Some of the remediation work may be critical to maintaining construction schedules. When
this occurs, the Engineer will establish a time for completion.
C. Hazardous Waste Management and Regulatory Compliance
1. Hazardous Waste Contingency Plan and Hazardous Waste Minimization Plan
a. If the location of the project is on a site which has a Large Quantity Generator
(LQG) status or subsequently obtains LQG status during the course of the Work,
then the Contractor shall conform to the requirements of the Hazardous Waste
Contingency Plan and Hazardous Waste Minimization Plan for that site.
2. Hazardous Waste Storage Compliance
a. The Contractor shall maintain compliance with hazardous waste storage
requirements at the W ork Site.  Storage areas and inspections of storage areas
must comply with the hazardous waste regulations detailed within 6 NYCRR
Parts 370 through 375.
3. Waste Management Records
a. Disposal of wastes generated by remediation Work will be based on the results of
testing and shall be at a site permitted to accept such waste by the U.S.
Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) or an authorized state or local
government agency. The Contractor shall provide remediation waste profiles for
DEP signature as generator, permit documentation required for the selected
Treatment, Storage, or Disposal Facility (TSDF) to receive these wastes, and the
transporter’s 6 NYCRR Part 364 Waste Transporter Permit(s) required to
transport wastes to the TSDF. The Contractor will conduct due diligence of the
TSDF, including a list of violations received. The Contractor shall submit a
facility letter of acceptance. The Contractor shall also provide advance copies of
the waste manifest(s) and Hazardous Waste Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR)
Notification and Certification Form(s) for the Engineer’s review and approval.
The Contractor will keep and update a Hazardous Waste Inventory Log and will
document weekly inspections by a Competent Person in the management of
hazardous waste.
b. On the date of shipment, t he Contractor shall submit copies of the completed
manifest, signed and dated by the initial transporter, in accordance with federal
and state requirements, to the DEP for signature. Copies of completed and signed
waste manifests from TSDF’s shall be provided to the Engineer as soon as
possible but no later than thirty (30) days of waste shipment offsite.
4. Changes to Hazardous Waste Generator Status
a. The Contractor shall be aware that work activities may result in a change to the
Work S ite’s hazardous waste generator status. Compliance with the revised
generator status is required.
5. Hazardous Waste Regulatory Program Fees and Taxes
455 of 772
SECTION 01 35 45 – HAZARDOUS MATERIALS CONTROL
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452 E
CAT-452  Section Page 6 of 22
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
a. The Contractor may be directed to pay the New York State Department of
Taxation and Finance for special assessments on hazardous waste generated at the
Project Site or the NYSDEC regulatory program fees charged to the facility
operating at the Site. When directed by the Engineer, the Contractor shall pay the
amount indicated within 48 hours of notification. The Contractor will be
reimbursed for the amount paid, with no provision for overhead and profit, from
the allowance provided for unforeseen hazardous m aterials remediation as
specified in Section 01 27 00 – Measurement and Payment.
1.06 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. Remediation plans for unforeseen hazardous materials shall comply with all applicable requirements
of federal, state, and local hazardous waste regulations and shall include, but not be limited to the
following:
1. Identification of hazardous and regulated/non-hazardous wastes associated with the Work.
2. Estimated quantities of wastes to be generated and disposed of.
3. Names and qualifications of each Subcontractor that will be testing, transporting, storing,
and disposing of wastes. Include the facility location and a 24 -hour telephone contact
number and applicable transporter and TSDF permits, EPA Identification Numbers, and
insurance certificates.
4. Names and qualifications (experience and training) of personnel who will be responsible
for on-site management of hazardous wastes.
5. Detailed description of the containment and removal procedures.
6. Spill prevention and cleanup contingency measures to be implemented.
7. Work plan for waste management, on-site storage, removal and disposal.
8. Detailed schedule indicating the beginning and completion dates for each activity and each
Work area, including time for clean-up, inspection, and monitoring activities.
1.07 SUBMITTALS
A. The following items shall be submitted as described above for the Engineer’s approval:
1. Initial Site Inspection Report and Summary Site Inspection Report
2. Remediation Plan for Unforeseen Hazardous Materials, when required.
3. Three (3) cost proposals from hazardous materials specialists for remedial action work,
when required.
4. EHASP upgrades as needed in accordance with Section 01 35 27 – Environmental, Health
and Safety Requirements.
B. The Contractor, when requested by the Engineer, shall provide additional copies of all reports and
related materials as may be needed for conferences with the Commissioner and other agencies
having jurisdiction.
1.08 DELIVERY, STORAGE, AND HANDLING
A. Not Used
1.09 SPARE PARTS, SPECIAL TOOLS, AND SUPPLIES
A. Not Used

PART 1 GENERAL
1.01 SUMMARY
A. Section Includes
1. Site Security Procedures
2. Site Video Surveillance
3. Security Guard Services
B. Contractor shall comply with all Site Security Procedures described herein.
C. The Contractor shall provide electronic safety and security equipment for physical
security, access control, intrusion detection and video surveillance purposes as
specified and directed by the Engineer.
D. The Contractor shall furnish personnel, uniforms, equipment and supplies necessary
to provide Security Guard Services at the Site, as described herein. The services
shall be provided as specified and as directed by the Engineer.
E. The following index of this Section is presented for convenience:

472 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 2 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
1. DEP Bureau of Police and Security Contractor Site Security Guidelines
⦁
1.02 PAYME
A. No separate payment shall be made for performing any Work required under this
Section.  All costs for Work required by this Section shall be included in the
applicable lump sum, unit price(s) or allowance(s) as set forth in Section 01 27 00 –
Measurement and Payment.
1.03 RELATED SECTIONS
A. Not Used
1.04 REFERENCES
A. Definitions
1. Visitor: A person visiting the project S ite(s) who is not employed by the
Department of Environmental Protection, New York Police Department,
Fire Department of New York, emergency medical services, the Contractor,
or Subcontracted to the Contractor.
1.05 DESCRIPTION
A. Site Video Surveillance
1. The Contractor shall provide S ite video surveillance to cover the project
area. Site video surveillance shall be provided 24 hours per day, 7 days per
week, 365 days per year.
2. Site video surveillance recordings and live viewing capability shall be
available via internet browser for the duration of the project and at the
direction of the Engineer.
3. Access to S ite video surveillance at all times shall be provided to the
Engineer, DEP Police Chief, and DEP Police Deputy Chief.
4. Site video surveillance shall be password protected.
a. Each user must have a unique user ID and password to login. Shared
or generic accounts are prohibited.
b. All user accounts should be standard accounts with the minimum
necessary access. Administrative access should only be granted
where absolutely required.
c. Passwords must be a minimum of 8 characters for regular account
and 15 characters for administrator privilege account, containing at
least one upper case letter, one lower case letter, one number and
one special character.
473 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 3 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
d. Login credentials must be  removed for any user whose
responsibilities on the project change, making it unnecessary to have
video access and/or those whose involvement with the project ends.
5. DEP will have the option to make S ite video surveillance equipment and
system permanent at end of construction.
B. Prior to the start of Work, the Contractor shall appoint a Security Point of Contact
(SPOC) to liaise with DEP Bureau of Police and Security (BPS) on all security
related matters.  An alternate SPOC (ASPOC) shall also be appointed by the
Contractor in the case of SPOC absence or unavailability. SPOC/ASPOC will be
responsible for ensuring Site security compliance.
C. Security Guard Service
1. The Contractor will be directed in writing by the Engineer as to the date of
commencement of the security services two (2) weeks prior to starting the
actual services.  Once the Security Guard Service starts, it shall remain in
continuous operation until project close-out or until directed by DEP.
2. The Contractor shall employ Security Guard Services specifically and
exclusively to safeguard the Site  and prevent access by unauthorized
persons in accordance with General Conditions, Article 9.  The Security
Services shall be provided 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per
year.  The Security Services shall include at least one (1) uniformed security
guard per shift.
3. Guards shall be stationed at the entrance to the site.
4. Guards shall be unarmed.
5. The se
curity guard shall be provided based on an 8-hour shift.  No security
guard shall be scheduled by the Contractor for more than twelve (12) hours
in any twenty- four (24) hour period, including placements at other
assignments.
6. The Contractor shall be responsible for the necessary supervision of the
guards and shall make unannounced visits to the property, for the purpose
of monitoring each guard’s performance.
a. Supervision of all security personnel must also be provided by the
entity that directly employs them.
7. All security personnel must pass a security clearance from the DEP Police
Detective Bureau and obtain a DEP Non- Employee Photo Identification
Card.  DEP Non -Employee Photo Identification Cards wi ll be issued by
DEP Police Detective Bureau or at the DEP Security Office, 96-05 Horace
Harding Expressway, Corona, NY.
a. Security clearances are valid for one year after which re -clearance
is required.
474 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 4 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
b. DEP Non -Employee ID Card must be worn on the outermost
garment and fully displayed by all security personnel while on Site.
c. New York State Guard Licenses must be in possession of the guard
while on duty and presented on demand when requested  by an
authorized person.
8. The Contractor shall ensure that meal breaks are provided for each guard
and shall provide a relief guard during the guard’s meal break.
a. This requirement may be waived by the Engineer during non-
working construction hours as long as the S ite can be satisfactorily
secured, i.e. gates/doors locked, interior alarm system activated.
b. Guard will be required to punch out upon taking break, and punch
in at the end of break.
9. Contractor and/or Security Guard Company shall have trained back-up
guards and supervisors available to ensure continuous, uninterrupted
service in the event a guard and/or supervisor is not present for duty.
10. All Work pursuant to this Contract shall be performed in compliance with
all applicable Federal, State, and local laws and regulations.
11. Security guard shall have a means of communications to contact 9-1-1, DEP
Police where applicable, and any emergency contacts that DEP may provide
at all times.
a. If a cell phone is used for communication , this designated phone
must remain with the post at all times while security coverage is
being performed.
12. If the Site security procedures require, in addition to performing security
functions, each security guard may be expected to perform  the duties of a
Fire Guard  and be certified as such per Section 1.06 (A)(2)(n).
D. Compliance with DEP Bureau of Police and Security Contractor Site Security
Guidelines
1. The Contractor shall comply with the DEP Bureau of Police and Security
Contractor Site Security Guidelines, attached at the end of this Section.
2. Security c learance or approval for temporary access shall be obtained from
DEP Bureau of Police and Security for all Contractor personnel working at
the Site(s) before personnel will be permitted to  access the Site.  Refer to
the DEP Bureau of Police and Security Contractor Site Security Guidelines
for:
a. Procedures required to obtain security c learance and associated
Security Clearance Form; and
b. Procedures required to obtain approval for temporary access and
associated Temporary Access Form.
475 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 5 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
1.06 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. Qualifications for Security Guards
1. The Contractor shall employ a Security Service Firm that is fully licensed
under New York State Law to provide the required security guards.  The
Contractor shall provide a copy of a valid New York State Guard Service
License prior to commencement of the Work.
a. The Security Guard Firm may not subcontract any of the Work.
b. The collaboration of two or more Security Guard companies as a
joint venture is permissible as long as t here is a common name and
uniform with an established single responsible person in charge or
all security personnel posted.
c. Comply fully with the New York State General Business Law
(GBL) Article 7 and 7-A Entitled Security Guard Act.
d. Possess a valid license issued by the New York State Department of
State (“NYSDOS License”), to act as a Private Investigator or
Watch Guard and Patrol Agency.
e. Be fully licensed under New York State Law General Business Law
Article 7, Section 70, and Licenses Paragraph Number 2 entitled
Private Investigators and Watch, Guard and Patrol Agencies, and
Article 8 entitled Process Server to provide the required security
guards. The Contractor shall provide a copy of the NYSDOS eleven
(11) digit unique identification number prior to commencement of
the Work.
f. The City will immediately terminate the Subcontract and Contractor
shall engage a new subcontractor  if the NYSDOS License is
suspended or revoked for any reason.  The Contractor cannot
substitute another Private Investigator and Watch, Guard or Patrol
Agency License if the Subcontractor’s NYSDOS license is
suspended or revoked.
g. The Security Service Firm shall have been engaged in the business
of providing uniformed security guard services for a minimum of
five (5) years and shall provide details of this experience prior to
commencement of the Work.
2. The Contractor shall ensure  that all assigned security guards are licensed
security guards registered under New York State law and meet the following
criteria:
a. Guards shall be at least eighteen (18) years of age.
b. Possess a high school diploma or possess a General Equivalency
Diploma.
476 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 6 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
c. Have at least one year of experience as a Uniformed Unarmed
Security guard including a minimum of 1,750 hours of service
preferably in a similar setting. This requirement can also be waived
by the BPS Security Division at its discretion or for candidates with
prior military service with an honorable discharge (DD214).
d. Possess a valid current New York State issued Driver License,
Learner’s Permit or Non-Driver I.D. card.
e. Have a current New York State Security Guard License
f. Possess and maintain valid Certification for the specific position to
which they are assigned. Some e xamples include: FDNY Handle
Motor Fuel-Gas Station P-15 Certification, Certificate of Fitness for
Fireguard Shelters F -44, or MARSEC (Maritime Security)/TWIC
Transportation Worker Identification Credential certifications.  The
Contractor is responsible for determining and complying with
correct certification.
g. Guards shall be able to understand, speak, read and write English
fluently.  They shall be able to intelligently communicate with
Visitors and prepare accurate written reports of any incidents.
h. All guards shall have undergone fingerprinting pursuant to New
York Department of State, Division of Licensing Services
regulations.
i. Guards shall be in excellent physical condition and shall supply
medical certification of fitness and good health prior to assignment.
This shall include an annual medical examination including a urine
drug screen test performed by a licensed testing laboratory approved
by the City.  The drug screen test shall test for the following
substances: amphetamines, barbiturates, benzodiazepines, cocaine,
marijuana, methadone, methaqualone, opiates, PCP’s propazphene.
j. Guards shall have no record of mental illness, history of residence
in a psychiatric institution, or history of drug or alcohol abuse.
k. Guards shall not have been convicted of a felony or other criminal
offense which in the NYS Department of State determination would
inhibit the guard’s ability to perform the duties of a security guard
in a reliable, competent and trustworthy manner in accordance with
the Contract Documents.
l. Guards shall perform their duties in full uniform.
m. Guards shall possess an OSHA Construction Safety  and Health
certificate (10 hour minimum)
n. Guards must have the appropriate Fire Guard Certification
applicable to the construction Site they are assigned to.  Contractor
477 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 7 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
is required to determine certification required by the local authority
having jurisdiction.  (Examples of such certifications include
Certificate of Fitness (CoF) F01 – Citywide Fire Guard for
Impairment, F02 - Fire Guard of Shelters, F60 – Fire Guard for
Torch Operations, F92 – Supervise and Fire Guard Boatyards and
Marinas).
3. Any security guard found not to meet the above qualifications shall be
immediately replaced within 24 hours upon the direction of the Engineer.
The replacement shall be made so that no interruption in security guard
coverage takes place.
B. Training
1. Contractor shall ensure that all guards employed under this Contract receive
training as per Article 7 -A, Section 89-n of the New York State General
Business Law and the New York State Security Guard Act of 1992.
2. All Site security personnel must have received the following training prior
to posting:
a. State Required training:
1) 8 Hour Pre-Assignment
2) 16 Hour OJT (on the job training)
3) Current 8 Hour Annual
b. Training must also include the following:
1) Proper confrontation to include procedures for courteous
reception and questioning of individuals; procedures to
restrict ingress and egress routes while maintaining a
professional bearing and decorum; and procedures to
conduct body and package searches in an appropriately  non-
aggressive and inoffensive manner.
2) Radio communication principals and radio use
3) Legal restriction of arrests, searches and seizures for New
York State
4) Use of force for New York State
5) Procedures to be followed in reporting a violation of law to
a duly constituted law enforcement agency
6) Procedures for reporting an unusual occurrence incident,
including familiarization of the incident report and the “who,
what, when, where and why” method of reporting
7) Recognition of security and safety hazards, and fire
protection and prevention including fire alarm procedures
478 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 8 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
8) Appropriate use of installed fire equipment and emergency
evacuation procedures
9) Emergency response and evacuation procedures;
10) Bomb threat procedures;
11) Fire alarm procedures and appropriate use of installed fire
equipment
12) Ethics and law
13) Identifying and reporting sexual harassment and
discrimination and workplace violence
14) Prohibition of Fraternizing with DEP’s and contractor’s staff
and visitors
15) Terrorism Awareness Training
16) Observation and detection of potential hostile surveillance
17) Operation of an x- ray machine
18) Operation of proper screening with a magnetic wand
19) Operation of a magnetometer (walk through metal detector)
20) Operation of electronic barriers and gate
21) Control center operations where applicable
3. Copies of all applicable training materials, including examinations, must be
provided anew to DEP whenever changed.
4. Records of training sessions must be maintained by the Contractor. Copies
of training session records must be sent to DEP Project Manager within
seventy-two (72) hours of the completion of training.
5. Prior to posting , all security guards must receive a site specific 2 -hour
orientation conducted by the DEP BPS Security Manager and/or authorized
representative(s).
a. This requirement may be waived on a site -by-site basis at the
discretion of DEP BPS Director of Security and/or appointed
authorized representative(s).
b. Subsequent security guards assigned to the S ite after the beginning
of security coverage shall receive a similar orientation by the
Contractor SPOC/ASPOC.  The Contractor must appoint a
“Trainer” to conduct orientations for future security personnel
assigned to the Site.
6. 2-Hour Orientation - Requirements
a. Security Personnel Requirements
479 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 9 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
1) Roster of 6 security guards shall be sent to initial training, to
account for future sick, vacation, resignations, etc. at the post
2) Attendees shall possess a of NYS Security Guard License
3) Inquire if any are active or retired Police Officers and of gun
possession
4) Authorized representative of security company  shall be  in
attendance
5) Prime Contractor SPOC or authorized representative shall be
in attendance
b. Other Requirements
1) Temporary guard booth shall be in place and powered.
2) 24- hour means to communicate with  911 and DEP Police
Ashokan, Eastview, or Hillview Precinct (if upstate or at
designated In-City sites)
3) Working flashlight must be available.
4) 24- hour contact information for Security Company  shall be
provided.
5) Means of time accountability  shall be in place , i.e., time
clock that is punched every hour and generates report.
7. 2-Hour Orientation Agenda must include at a minimum:
a. Introduction
b. General Requirements:
1) Expectations and responsibilities for security officers
2) Review of requirements
c. Specific Post Orders
1) Requirements of the site(s)
2) Patrol / Access Control Procedures
3) What to look for
4) How to contact DEP Police (at sites where applicable)
5) Use of Log Book
6) Recording Key Punches
7) Reporting Spills
8) Evacuations
d. Tour of Site(s):
480 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 10 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
1) Familiarization of post and surrounding area
e. Distribution of Security Post Orders:
1) Contractor Security Point of Contact (SPOC)/(ASPOC)
2) Authorized Security Company Representative
3) Post
f. Acknowledgement Statement Form : U pon the completion of the
orientation, the following must sign the form:
1) Security Guard(s)
2) Authorized Security Company Representative
3) Contractor (SPOC)/(ASPOC)
a) The originals shall be submitted to the Engineer.
8. Annual Training Requirements:
a. 8-Hour Annual In- Service Training (all security personnel both
armed and unarmed)
b. 8- Hour Annual Firearms Training (all armed security personnel)
C. Qualifications for Security Guard Supervisors
1. In addition to meeting all the qualification requirements of a Uniformed
Unarmed Security Guard, Supervisors shall meet all of the following
qualifications:
a. Be at least 21 years old
b. Have at least one year of supervisory experience in any field; and
c. Have a minimum of two (2) years of additional experience in any
one or any combination of the following:
1) Experience as a security guard with a documented successful
work history
2) Experience as an armed guard/watchman
3) Experience in the Armed Forces, Military Reserves,
National Guard or as a State Guard, with honorable
discharge or equivalent
4) Experience as a probation officer, corrections officer, police
officer, or park ranger, attendance at an accredited institution
of higher learning in criminal justice, police science or a
related field (completion of 24 credits may be substituted for
one year of required service.
D. Standards of Conduct
481 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 11 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
1. Contractor(s) shall ensure and certify that all assigned staff complies with
the following:
a. Meet the requirements of all federal, state and local laws, including
the N.Y.S. General Business Law § 70 et seq. and § 89-e et seq., and
any successor legislation, including but not limited to, the
satisfactory completion of the specified fingerprint checks
b. Have no physical conditions that would hamper the performance of
assigned duties
c. Be able to stand for a minimum of four (4) consecutive hours
d. Be free from communicable disease
e. Hear within normal speech range and volume
f. Possess a valid social security number
g. Not be under indictment for any felony or any other offense
specified in the N.Y.S. General Business Law §§ 81 or 89-h
h. Be physically able to wear and to be properly trained in the use of
all protective equipment
i. Possess mental alertness and emotional stability
j. Have passed a background investigation conducted by the
Contractor(s), consisting of: FBI fingerprint check; seven (7) year
prior employers check; out-of-state criminal records check; personal
references check; New York State criminal background checks; and
driver’s license check
k. Have tested negative, both prior to employment and on an annual
basis thereafter, to random Controlled Substance Use (CSU) testing,
for the presence of the following substances and/or their metabolites
on a clinical drug test administered by a lab licensed by the State of
New York to administer such examinations: amphetamines;
barbiturates; benzodiazepines; cocaine; cannabinoids; methadone;
methamphetamines; opiates; phencyclidine; propoxyphene; and
tricyclic antidepressants.  Copies of the test results must be placed
in the employee’s personnel file and be provided to the User Agency
upon request.
l. Annual CSU testing required under these Contract(s) will be of a
random nature, whereby CSU testing is carried out each year on a
random date, without prior notification to the respective employee
or their supervisor.
m. All CSU testing will be conducted in accordance with all legal
standards, as required under Local, State and/or Federal laws for the
industry.

n. All Security Personnel testing positive will be immediately removed
from their post and eliminated from any future assignments under
this Contract.
o. In the event a security guard is required to ingest prescription
medication, it is the Contractor's responsibility to ensure that the
guard is fit for duty and that the appropriate documentation is made
available to the DEP. Such medication must be carried  in the
original prescription container, with the pharmacist's label affixed
thereto. specifying the pharmacy name, prescription number,
doctor's name, drug name and control number. Additionally, it is the
Contractor's responsibility to ensure that guards are not assigned for
duty while under the influence of any medication that could impair
their ability to carry a firearm and/or perform required job duties.

1.07 SUBMITTALS
A. Within 14 days after the Notice to Proceed, the Contractor shall submit to the
Engineer the Security Surveillance Plan for approval.
B. Within thirty (30) days of the Notice to Proceed, the Contractor shall submit to the
Engineer the Security Service Firm for approval.
C. Within twenty-four (24) hours of retaining the Security Service Firm, the Contractor
shall be required to maintain a personnel folder on each security guard employed
under this Contract.  Only guards for whom the following information is submitted
may be assigned.  The folder shall contain the following information:
1. Guard’s name
2. Home address
3. Date of birth
4. Duplicate photo ID card
5. Social security number
6. Copy of employment application
7. Copy of New York State Division of Licensing Application Form 307
8. Copy of birth certificate
9. Copy of high school diploma or General Equivalency Diploma
10. Military record (if applicable)
11. Documentation of prior employment
12. Copy of New York State fingerprint card
13. Record of security training , pursuant to the Security Guard Act to include
8-hour annual training for the duration of the Contract.

CAT-452 Section Page 13 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
14. Medical certification of fitness and good health
a. Contractor and/or Security Guard Company shall ensure that all
guards and supervisors undergo and pass a physical exam
administered by a physician licensed to practice medicine in the
State of New York. Such physical exams shall take place no earlier
than twenty (20) days before Contractor and/or Security Services
Company hires such guard or supervisor.
b. Contractor and/or Security Services Company shall further ensure
that all guards and supervisors currently employed by them,
successfully pass an annual post hiring medical examination
administered by a physician licensed to practice medicine in New
York State. Such annual physical exams shall take place within (30)
days of the anniversary date of the prior physical.
15. Drug screen test results for the past 12 months or since employment ,
whichever is shorter.
16. Result, if any, of criminal record check.
17. Proof of successfully passed security clearance from DEP BPS  Police
Detective Unit.
D. The above referenced files shall be made accessible and delivered to the Engineer
within twenty-four (24) hours of the request.
1.08 DELIVERY, STORAGE, AND HANDLING
A. Reporting
1. All unusual occurrences that require a security guard to take official action
must be reported immediately to the police and the Engineer as appropriate.
2. It is the responsibility of the Contractor to keep all reports in a binder
according to location.
B. Site Security
1. Additional Security
a. Should the Contractor consider the security requirements outlined
above inadequate, it may provide such additional security as it
deems necessary.  The additional cost of such approved increased
protection shall be paid by the Contractor.
b. Nothing contained herein shall diminish in any way the
responsibility of the Contractor for safeguarding and protecting its
own personnel, work, materials, tools and equipment.
c. Warning signs, lanterns and other devices shall be provided and
maintained as required for protection of the public and Site working

included by the Contractor in the price bid for the Contract.

B. Security Guards
1. Uniform and Appearance
a. The Contractor shall furnish distinctive and appropriate complete
uniforms to all security guards and supervisory security personnel
at no cost to DEP . All assigned personnel must have standard,
matching uniforms. All supervisors’ uniforms must be distinctive
from guards’ uniforms, (i.e., contain different color shirt than guard)
and contain rank insignia.
b. The uniform and appearance shall be as follows:
1) Uniform shirts (long sleeve or short sleeve, depending on
weather conditions).  The Contractor(s)’ shoulder patch shall
be on the sleeves and a security square badge with name
plate shall be worn on the front
2) Uniform trousers;
3) A black tie (snap or button type only);

4) Black shoes
5) For staff assigned indoors, a uniform blazer with the
Contractor’s shoulder patch on the left breast pocket and a
metal badge with name plate to be worn on the front;
6) For staff assigned outdoors, a uniform jacket with the
Contractor’s shoulder patch on the sleeves and a metal badge
with name plate to be worn on the front, and a cap with badge
(baseball style caps are acceptable) – the cap must be wor n
front-facing and “squared,” not tilted or slanted.
7) Appropriate clothing for inclement weather, i.e. rain boots,
rain coat, winter hat (must clearly identify the wearer as a
security guard).
8) Appropriate safety equipment that site requires in
performance of duties, i.e. hard hat, safety vest, safety
goggles, steel toed boots (must clearly identifies the wearer
as a security guard.)
c. All items of uniform shall be maintained in a clean, well pressed
condition.
d. Contractor must provide appropriate fitting uniform for guards who
are pregnant.
2. Equipment
a. Working flashlight – with replacement batteries available
b. Post Log Book
1) Contractor shall provide log  books according to
specifications: 8.5 inches by 14 inches hard covered bound
with lined pages inside
2) Must be present and remain at security post.
3) All log entries made by security officers must be in
permanent black ink.  Use of correction fluid such as Wite -
Out is not permitted.  Mistakes in entries are to have a single
line drawn through them and initialed.
4) Security Supervisors and those charged with overseeing
security at a site (SPOC/ASPOC) must sign their names and
entry and exit times in the logbook in red ink.
5) Log books shall be continuously maintained by Contractor’s
personnel and shall contain the following chronological
entries:
a) Signature of each employee coming on duty

C. Communication Equipment
1. Contractor shall possess device systems for use at each site as defined. In
any facility or location where two or more guards are assigned to work
simultaneously, the Contractor shall provide portable hand- held two-way
radios for use by each guard.  Contractor shall also provide appropriate
training and accessories such as batteries, chargers, and carrying case.  Such
radios shall be fully operational at all times. The radio must be fully charged
at the beginning of each shift.  Such radios shall be licensed by the Federal
Communication Commission (FCC) on a business or public safety band.
Such radios and accessories shall be provided in sufficient quantities to
ensure that there is at least one operable, fully charged radio and case for
each guard and supervisor on each shift. DEP must approve all equipment
prior to use.
2. DEP’s BPS Deputy Commissioner and/or their authorized representative(s)
may also require that one or more radios be made available to the DEP’s
management personnel, at no cost to DEP, to maintain contact with the
Contractor’s security guards on S ite and to monitor their radio
transmissions. Where radios are required for the DEP’s personnel, such
radios must be made to operate on the same frequency as that assigned to
the guards and will include cases and fully charged operable radios on each
shift. Upon completion of service, such radios will be returned to the
Contractor.
3. In addition, Contractor shall supply least one (1) working cellular phone
capable of transmitting and receiving calls at an assigned post for use by
guards on duty. Contractor’s personnel shall provide the phone number for
the post cell phones to the DEP before each shift. All expenses associated
with the use of cellular phones are to be assumed by the Contractor.
489 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 19 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
4. At Sites with no radio or cellular communication capability, Contractor
shall provide a communication methodology to meet the DEP minimum
requirements for guards to contact 911 and necessary notifications at no
additional cost to the City.
5. All communication equipment must be in good operating condition and
shall be supplied with a necessary back-up source.
6. The communications equipment must be tested regularly to ensure its
functionality at the S ite. If not functioning, the Contractor is required to
replace it within four hours. Testing and equipment replacement must be
noted in the post log book.
D. Electronic Timekeeping/Guard Tour System
1. Provide Timekeeping/Guard Tour system (electronic, turnkey acceptable if
conditions do not warrant electronic) that is accessible to DEP BPS Security
Division when requested.
2. Must be able to operate in an all-weather environment.
3. Records of all tours are the property of DEP and must be made available
and accessible to DEP Project Manager and BPS Security Division when
requested.

3.02 APPLICATION
A. Security Guard Duties and Responsibilities
1. Security guards shall use discretion, courtesy, tact, and necessary force
when protecting DEP locations.
2. Reporting to Post – all security guards reporting to post shall:
a. Arrive at scheduled starting time neat and professional in
appearance.
b. Be in complete uniform displaying company logo.

SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E

c. If arriving at post for first-time contact the most senior DEP person
on Site responsible for security or Contractor SPOC/ASPOC
d. Obtain any other necessary information from prior shift.
e. Review Post Log Book from previous shift and print their name.
Identify and be familiar with:
1) Summary of incidents that took place the previous shift
2) Ongoing situations
3) Actions pending
4) Special instructions
5) Check all required post equipment to make sure it is in
working order.  Report to most senior DEP person on S ite
responsible for security, prime contractor SPOC/ASPOC,
and your company supervisor any equipment not working.
Enter all information in Post Log Book.
f. Safeguard all keys and equipment used during the normal course of
their duties:
1) A working phone both landline and/or cellular or Nextel
2) A working two-way radio if used
3) A working flashlight
4) Whistle
5) Log Book
6) Pen(s)
7) All job-related contract information including postings
8) Any other equipment assigned related to job duties, i.e. keys
3. Post Assignment:
a. Be familiar with all site-specific post orders (can be found at security
post)
b. Follow Log Book procedures as outlined.
c. Shall protect persons or property from:
1) Harm
2) Theft
3) Other unlawful activity
d. Respond to a security system alarm
e. Observe, detect, and report unlawful or unauthorized activity

including:
1) Employees
2) Contractors
3) Visitors
4) Vehicles
5) Traffic
g. Enforce security policies, rules, regulations and procedures.
h. Areas of responsibilities can include:
1) Site buildings
2) Existing assets
3) Trailers
4) Grounds
5) Appurtenances, and occupants
i. Protect areas of responsibility against:
1) Fire
2) Vandalism
3) Theft
4) Loitering
5) Trespass
6) Burglaries/Unlawful Entry
7) Bodily harm
8) Building systems malfunctions and deficiencies (water
leaks, open windows, lights out)
9) Hostile surveillance
10) Terrorism threats
j. Assist in emergency evacuations, both drill and actual – know
evacuation routes and primary/secondary assembly areas.
k. Respond to emergencies, summoning assistance and obtaining
professional assistance, in accordance with DEP Site procedures, in
the event of injury or illness to anyone on the protected premises
l. Assist DEP and public safety personnel in case of any emergency
such as fire, bomb threat, assault, or civil disturbance.

m. Prepare reports to be submitted to DEP on accidents, fires, thefts,

n. Perform other such duties as may be prescribed such as:
1) Attend to parking facilities
2) Escort duties
3) Observe/ screen Visitors
4) Issue passes
5) Check IDs
6) Operating vehicle barrier gates, magnetometers, security x -
ray machines and handheld security wands
7) Controlling gates, doors and lighting as required
o. Conduct patrols as required and dictated by site-specific post orders.
p. Upon intrusion by an unidentified person, Security Guards shall
make an inquiry as to the authorization to enter the premises and
take the necessary action as follows:
1) Provide the individual with temporary identification, or
2) Request the intruder to vacate the premises, or
3) If a threatening situation or criminal activity occurs, notify
911 and DEP Police (if location is upstate or at a designated
In-City Site) and other contacts as proscribed by site specific
post orders.
q. Security guards going off duty shall  pass along all relevant
information and instructions to the relieving security guard prior to
leaving any post.
r. A post is never to be left unattended without official permission
except if at end of tour and no relief is scheduled.
s. Access control:  Follow Site specific access control policies:
1) Identify all persons entering and leaving Agency
construction locations and may be required to operate X-ray
or scanning equipment in the course of maintaining access
control, including the inspection of bags, packages, parcels,
etc.
2) Perform vehicle inspections (interior, trunk, undercarriage,
etc.) during heightened security alerts, or when instructed to
do so.  Any vehicle entering or exiting a DEP site is subject
to a search.

SPOC/ASPOC

SPOC/ASPOC.
t. Report all irregularities, suspected irregularities, hazards, and

2) Immediately report all safety violations to DEP Project

a) This includes vehicles, people, sounds, smells, and

u. Press Inquiries / Requests for Information – Do not speak to any

y. Cooperate fully with DEP and law enforcement in regard to any

4. Provide name, company, and location while reporting anything or

5. Upon completion of shift immediately leave the Site and do not remain in

B. Prohibited Post Behavior
1. While not an exhaustive list, below are examples of behaviors and item
possession that could subject the security guard to immediate and prompt
removal from the Contract and even arrest and criminal prosecution.
a. Reporting to work intoxicated or under the  influence of any illegal
drugs
b. Consuming or possessing alcohol or illegal drugs
c. Smoking inside Security Booth or onsite except in designated
smoking areas
d. Being in possession of any non- job related issued weapon, even if
legal.  No firearms are permitted on DEP Property even with
carrier’s permit (exception are appropriately issued weapons to
armed guards that are formally approved)
e. Sleeping of any kind or other inattentive behavior
f. Being in non-designated areas of the Site and / or unauthorized use
to Site equipment
g. Being on Site when not on duty; guard must vacate Site when shift
ends
h. Having personal visitors
i. Making personal phone calls except in an emergency
j. Leaving post without proper notification and authorization
k. Failure to follow job-related directions
l. Possessing or operating any device that distracts from post
responsibilities
m. Plugging in of any non -job issued electr onic equipment to a S ite
outlet
n. Revealing details about security procedures or unusual in cidents to
unauthorized persons
o. Speaking to the press or unauthorized persons
496 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 26 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
p. Any other conduct or items possessed not listed above that  might
compromise job performance.
C. Fire Guard Duties and Responsibilities
1. Fire Guards’ responsibilities include reducing the threat of fires in a variety
of locations within a facility.  They are also responsible for ensuring that all
fire safety regulations are obeyed.
2. Fire Guards are required when fire sprinklers are not installed or when fire
sprinklers are malfunctioning, including at construction Sites.
3. Fire Guards must be present when an automatic fire protection system is
shut down while being repaired.
4. Fire guards shall have ability to manually operate each alarm station on the
premises.
5. Duties include, but not limited to:
a. Assist in the evacuation of the location in the event of fire or other
critical situation.
b. Provide clear and concise instructions to building occupants to
ensure safe and orderly evacuations.
c. In the case of a fire, responsible for activating the fire alarm;
notifying the Fire Department a nd, if applicable, the facility’s Fire
Safety Director.
d. Direct fire fighters to the scene of the fire.
D. Security Guard Supervisor’s Duties and Responsibilities
1. Supervisors shall direct and monitor the performance of security guards at
the Sites, as directed by DEP, to ensure that security guards are performing
their duties in accordance with the Contract.
2. Supervisors assigned under the Contract shall be the person designated, in
writing, to have the authority and training to supervise all levels of
employees on a day-to-day basis.
3. The Contractor and/or the Security Services Company shall be responsible
for the necessary supervision of the Guards and shall make unannounced
visits to the properties for the purpose of monitoring each guard’s
performance.  A Supervisor’s Report, summarizing their findings (including
times and dates of visit), shall be submitted to the DEP Project Manager on
a monthly basis.
4. The Contractor and/or Security Services Company shall provide a
supervisor who shall randomly visit the Site at least once per week
alternating between all three shifts. The supervisor shall sign in the log book
in red ink at the security post provided upon arrival, the DEP visitor log in
497 of 772
SECTION 01 35 55 – SITE SECURITY
CONTRACT  CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 27 of 28
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
the DEP Trailer, and communicate with DEP Project Manager.  The
supervisor will ensure all employees are present, attentive to their duties,
and in conformance with the requirements of the Contract.
5. Supervisors shall make unannounced inspections of guards at the Site a
minimum of at least once within a twenty -four (24)-hour rotation in order
to ensure that the guards are acting in conformance with terms and
conditions of the Contract.
6. Supervisors shall monitor to ensure that the guards are properly uniformed,
alert and aware of and performing the required duties.
7. Supervisors shall not simultaneously perform the duties of security guard.
8. A written report, to include the date, time, and results of supervisory
inspections shall be transmitted electronically to DEP Project Manager
within three (3) days following each inspection.  Supervisors shall note their
times of arrival and departure in the log book provided by DEP as well as
in their Memo Book and must punch in and out on a  time clock, if/when
required.
E. Rejection/Dismissal of Staff
1. Designated officials of DEP (Project Manager and/or authorized
representative(s) may reject staff for any reason.
2. DEP may reject, dismiss or request removal of any personnel supplied by
the Contractor and/or Security Guard Company from their assignment by
notifying the Contractor.  Upon such notification, Contractor shall be
required to remove the employee(s) immediately or by such time as is
designated by DEP.
3. Any guard or supervisor who has been rejected by DEP shall not be
redeployed to any other DEP Site without written permission of BPS
Security Division.
4. Guards and supervisors dismissed from duty must be replaced within four
(4) hours of dismissal.
5.
3.03 FIELD TESTING / QUALITY CONTROL
A. Recordkeeping
1. Contractor(s) shall maintain business records pursuant to Rules and
Regulations of Title 19 NYCRR, Section 170.8, entitled Business Records
for Private Investigator, Watch, Guard and Patrol Agencies.  The personnel
record for each employee assigned to work  hereunder must contain the
following and be available for review upon request by DEP.
2. Name and home address

3. Employment application
4. Date of birth
5. Unique employee identification number
6. Color photograph (from photo ID card)
7. Birth certificate
8. High school diploma or GED
9. NYS fingerprint card
10. Record of basic training as required by the NYS Security Guard Act of
1992; FDNY; etc.
11. Medical exam certificate
12. Clinical drug test results to include urine analysis
13. Results of annual drug screening
14. Results of background investigation
15. Color copy of a valid current armed security guard license issued by the
State of New York (where applicable)
16. Color copy of a valid current security guard license issued by the State of
New York
17. Color copy of a valid current New York State driver’s license, or New York
State Non-Driver ID Card for those security guards not operating a motor
vehicle or who do not have a Driver License
18. Pursuant to the Title 19 NYCRR, Section 170.5, entitled “Principal
Business Address,” all Contractors shall maintain a principal place of
business within New York State, which shall contain the employee and
business records for any licensed activities cond ucted within New York
State. A residence may not be considered as a principal business address.
19. The Contractor must provide above records to DEP Project Manager and
DEP BPS Security Division upon request.

PART 1 GENERAL
1.01 SUMMARY
A. This Section describes the Envision requirements of this Project. Work includes
environmental and sustainable practice requirements related to the Institute for
Sustainability Infrastructure’s (ISI) Envision Rating System. A goal of this Project
is to construct a facility that meets ISI’s Envision Rating System.
B. The intent is for Envision goals to be achieved in a manner that ultimately provides
a safe and healthy environment for building occupants and community members
with minimal negative impact on the local, regional and global environment.
C. Attachments
1. Attachment A: Sustainability Construction Envision Documentation
2. Attachment B: Environmental Materials Reporting Form
D. The following index of this Section is presented for convenience:

Section. All costs for Work required by this Section shall be included in the

1.04 REFERENCES
A. ISI’s Envision Version 3 (V3) as released on April 17, 2018, is the Envision
framework used and referenced herein.
B. “Pathway A” refers to the Envision V3 compliance pathway being utilized for this
Project. Pathway A includes a submission to ISI during 90% Design and a follow-
up submission to ISI Post -Construction. The first submission is herein referred to
as the ISI Design Submission.
1.05 DESCRIPTION
A. General
1. The Contractor shall comply with sustainability requirements as indicated
in this Section, other Specifications and Contract Drawings. The Contractor
shall  meet all sustainability req uirements as stipulated in Attachment A
through the New Material Sustainable Implementation Plan, and the
Envision Evidence Documentation.
2. Contractor role within Envision certification:
a. The Contractor shall develop a New Material Implementation Plan
and the Envision Evidence Document, which will be submitted the
Design Engineer for use as  Evidence to support a “Gold”
certification.
501 of 772
SECTION 01 35 63 – SUSTAINABILITY CERTIFICATION PROJECT
REQUIREMENTS
CONTRACT CAT-452-G AND CAT-452-E
CAT-452 Section Page 3 of 10
Rehabilitation of Five (5) Catskill Aqueduct Chambers
b. The ISI Design Submission is generated by the Engineer and
included in the bid exhibit documents. The ISI Design Submission
is expressly excluded from and is not a part of this Contract and is
available for information purposes only.
c. The Contractor shall submit evidence as listed in Attachment A -
Sustainability Construction Envision Documentation.  The Contract
Documents are not intended to limit alternative means of achieving
Envision requirements.   Suggestions from the construction team
during Project meetings for implementing  sustainability goals are
encouraged.
3. All new materials shall be procured in accordance with the Environmentally
Preferred Purchasing guidelines per Local Laws 118-121 of 2005.
1.06 QUALITY ASSURANCE
A. The Contractor shall have an Envision Qualified Professional on staff who shall be
responsible for Envision issues compliance and coordination.
1. The Envision Qualified Professional  shall be an Envision Sustainability
Professional ( ENV SP ). The Envision Qualified Professional  shall be
responsible for sustainability considerations described in this section
including the New Materials Sustainable Implementation Plan and the
Envision Evidence Documentation.
1.07 SUBMITTALS
A. New Materials Sustainable Implementation Plan
1. The Contractor shall develop and implement the New Materials Sustainable
Implementation Plan for the Project, where new materials refer to any item,
supply, equipment, or other physical object being newly purchased for the
single-use or long-term use for the duration of the Project.
2. The New Materials Sustainable Implementation Plan shall provide a
method for how the Contractor plans to meet the requirements stipulated in
Attachment A for recycled material, regional material, and sustainabl y
sourced content.
3. The Contractor shall prepare and submit the New Materials Sustainable
Implementation Plan for review and approval by the Engineer thirty (30)
days after receipt of Notice to Proceed and prior to the selection of any new
materials.
4. The Contractor shall use the Sustainable Material Content Plan (SMCP) as
reference for the development of the New Materials Sustainable
Implementation Plan. The SMCP is generated by the Engineer and included

in the bid exhibit documents. The SMCP is expressly excluded from and is
not a part of this Contract and is available for information purposes only.
